details.text: - These are from the Huroof Al-Muqatta’aat (الحُرُوفُ المُقَطَّعَات), letters which are cut up from one another. The majority opinion is that their meaning is not known except by Allah.
Derivative: هُدًى (guidance) implies to direction here i.e., something that will guide him upon the correct path or in the right direction.
Derivative: مَكَثَ means to stay in wait in anticipation of something.
Derivative: قَبَسٌ is a borrowed flame that you use to make a light or torch for yourself.
Derivative: آنَسَ comes from أُنْس which means to have a have an affiliation implying closeness or intimacy. Another meaning of آنَسَ is to be very observant of hints and keen on drawing conclusions.
- This structuring of this phrase has indications that Musa only saw hints of a fire which he was certain that he saw but was not visible for everyone else to see.
Derivative: أَعْرَضَ and إِعْراض doesn’t just imply ignoring but rather it means recognising something as being present but then turn away to avoid it.

Derivative: حِمْلٌ refers to the burden that is the most that you can possibly carry meaning the ultimate load that keeps getting piled on top of until it reaches the maximum amount that can be endured.
Derivative: زُرْقًا means the colour blue and refers to the changing of the colour of their eyes due to the severity of what they face in terms of extreme fear and horror on that day.
Derivative: يَتَخَافَتُونَ means that they will be whispering and talking quietly to one another.
- أَمْثَلُهُمْ طَرِيقَةً means the most exemplary of them in terms of lifestyle or manner of living or status and the most knowledgeable from among them.
Derivative: يَنسِفُ means to break them into small parts and collapse them or pulverize them to dust.
Derivative: To be levelled, smoothed and made flat.
- It means an endless desert or (barren) wasteland that is complete flat with no rises or dips.
Derivative: خَشَعَ means to be overwhelmed and subdued and in relation to voices it means to be silenced and made quiet (due to the fear of Allah).
Derivative: هَمْس refers to the low sounds of footsteps that sounds like whispers.
- there will be no عِوَجَ crookedness to it which means there will be no diverting away from it.
Derivative: شَفَاعَة means for someone to make a case and intercede on your behalf.
- Two meanings here: 1) He (Allah) will be pleased with what he has to say and 2) And that He (Allah) will be pleased with the fact that he (specifically) is the one who is speaking.
Derivative: فَلَمَّآ (Then when …) usually implies that it took some time.
Derivative: يُحِيطُ means to encompass and encircle.
Derivative: In a state of wretchedness and dejection.
- For faces to be hanging low, to be hung down in shame and to be humbled.
Derivative: هَضْم refers to a shortage or being decreased. Here in respect to his deeds.
- The inclusion of مِنْ (from) indicates to a portion of the صالحات (righteous or good deeds).
Derivative: أَحْدَثَ – يُحْدِثُ – إِحْدَاث – refers to something new happening or something would happen as if it is the first time.

Derivative: صَرَّفْنَا from صَرَّفَ – تَصْريف: To alternate and find multiple and various ways (i.e., to show or explain something). It is said in Arabic: تَصْرِيف الرِّياح meaning: The winds (الرِّياح) blow in alternate, various and different directions – this way and that way.
Derivative: الوَعِيد means the promised and threatened warning.

allah has provided various ways of demonstrating (صَرَّفْنَا) his promise and threat (الوَعِيد) so that they might protect themselves and have taqwa (يَتَّقُونَ) and so that they may be reminded from anew or the reminder comes to them in a way that they feel that it’s the first time that they are hearing it (يُحْدِثُ لَهُمْ ذِكْرًا).

- Two possible meanings: 1) That we did find for him the power of resolve i.e., to maintain what he had been commanded with, and 2) That we did not find in him deliberateness i.e., that he forgot, and it was not a deliberate act.
Derivative: تَشْقَى to mean to be put into difficulty, toil and distress.

Derivative: سَوْءَة refers to a spiritual ugliness linked to shame and disgraceful acts. It has been used to refer to a corpse in the Qur’an due to its ugliness and it being resultant from a disgraceful shameful act (سَوْءَةَ أَخِيهِ), when one of the sons of Adam killed his brother.
Derivative: To immediately start to do something.
Derivative: غَوَى means to be way off track or off the path.
Derivative: To lace together and stick one thing on top of another.
Derivative: اِجْتَبَى means to choose with the implication that the choice is made based on seeing good in the one being chosen.
Derivative: ثُمَّ means “then” but implies some time later as opposed to straight away after.
Derivative: To be a failure, be in s state of wretchedness or overburdened.
Derivative: ضَنكًا from ضَنَكَ - يَضْنُكُ which means to be very tight, confined and constricted i.e., referring here to a very uncomfortable and untranquil life.
Derivative: أَعْرَضَ to deliberately ignore (or turns away from).
Derivative: أَسْرَفَ means to go too far, over the top, exceed all bounds and be excessive.
- It refers to those of sharp intellect who can pursue things or matters to their conclusion.
Derivative: لُزُوم refers to something that adheres and clings. Here it means that the punishment and destruction would have been attached and stuck to them by right.

- أَجَلٌ مُّسَمًّى means a time (أَجَلٌ) that is very well named or has a lot of names (مُّسَمًّى), referring to the Day of Judgement. مُّسَمًّى (named) can also mean the time that has been fixed and set.
Derivative: اِخْتَارَ – اِخْتِيار (to choose) comes from خَيْر (Good) in Arabic and contains the meaning of I have seen something good in you and that’s why I chose you.

Derivative: أَزْوَاج here means categories and groups that are comparable to each other and dependent upon to one another, which can refer to the elite and influential groups within society which support one another.
Derivative: زَهْرَةَ means glitz and glamour.
Derivative: تَمُدَّ from مَدَّ to extend and stretch
Derivative: خِزْيٌ means to lose respect in the sight of others, to be discredited and humiliated.
Derivative: ذِلَّةٌ means to lose power and to be subdued by a higher power.
Derivative: سَوِيّ comes from سَوَى which means to be equally distant from both extremes, that lies right in the middle.
Derivative: To be provided with complete compensation. الجَزاء in Arabic means complete and full compensation.
- No doubt about it, the (final) hour is coming. The use of آتِيَةٌ in the اِسْم الفاعِل (noun of the doer) form implies that its around the corner.
- “I am almost (أَكَادُ) hiding it” which implies that He will let some signs of it becoming known.
Derivative: رَدِيَ – يَرْدى literally means to fall off a cliff or a high place which means that you will fall into failure and ruin.
Derivative: يَسُدُّ to distract and obstruct.
Derivative: هَوَى refers to empty desires and blindly following whims.

- The use of يَٰمُوسَىٰ (O Musa) here at the end of the question is an expression of endearment here to make Musa at ease, in contrast to the first time it was used when he was first called by Allah, which was to grab his attention.

Derivative: هَشَّ is related to هَزَّ in Arabic which means to shake, however, هَشَّ is used when something soft is shaken whilst هّزَّ is when something substantial is shaken. Here, it refers to shaking bushes so that leaves fall for his sheep to eat.
Derivative: مَآرِب comes from إِرْب or أَرِب which means a critical need. مَآرِب refers to essential critical tasks.
Derivative: أَلْقِ from أَلْقى means to throw but just in front of you, like dropping something down before yourself.
- again, يَٰمُوسَىٰ was used here following the command as a means of comforting to make Musa (as) feel At ease.
Derivative: شَقَى - يَشْقَى - شَقاوَة in Arabic means to be exhausted, worn out, to be overwhelmed, overworked or be put into distress.

- The difference between “We sent down عَلَيْكَ (upon you)” and “We sent down إِلَيْكَ (to you)” is that عَلَى implies responsibility i.e., that We have revealed it as a responsibility and burden upon you.

Derivative: فَإِذَا
- فَإِذَا means immediately and unexpectedly.
Derivative: حَيَّةٌ is a snake the fangs of which are visible.
Derivative: سِيرَة refers to all the features of something i.e., the staff of Musa will be made to return to its prior form with all of its features just as it was before he threw it down.
Derivative: ضَمَّ means to make as one and to bring together.

- Literally means to open my chest for me. One meaning is to provide me with confidence. Another meaning is to make me fully in tune with this task where everything to do with this matter my mind is in tune with it and focused upon it. Another possible meaning is to make me completely calm, at peace and ease in this task where I am not obstructed by nervousness, fear or laziness etc.
- Literally means to untie the knot from my tongue. Its wider meaning is to remove any impediment from me that will prevent me from being about to speak and convey clearly.
Derivative: To fully or deeply understand.

Derivative: وَزِير comes from the word وِزْر which means a huge burden and responsibility. وَزِير is someone who takes a huge burden and responsibility off of you i.e., someone to assist him in his huge responsibility and burden.
Derivative: خَشِيَ - يَخْشَى - الخَشْيَة (fear) in the Arabic language this is a form of fear; an overwhelming fear, a fear than can paralyse you.
Derivative: To reinforce and provide strength to.
Derivative: أَزْر means: My side or back.
- بَصِيرًا بِ – means to watch over us i.e., to make sure that we have survived and been preserved.

Derivative: سُؤْل means مَسْؤول (that which has been asked for in addition to that which is needed i.e., in order to fulfil your mission), just like خُبْز (bread) is مَخْبُوز (that which has been baked).

Derivative: مَنٌّ in Arabic means a huge weight as a noun. As a verb مَنَّ it means to grant a favour. By combining the two meanings you attain: A favour that is very heavy. “We have provided you with a huge favour”.
Derivative: ٱلْيَمِّ refers to an extra wide river.
Derivative: صِناعَة in Arabic means to build or manufacture something piece by piece.

Derivative: مَحَبَّةً
- The Arabic language has two words for love from حَبَّ. They are حُبٌّ (Hubb) and مَحَبَّةٌ (Mahabbah). مَحَبَّةٌ (Mahabbah) is only mentioned once in the Qur’an. It is a مَصْدَر مِيمي (Masdar Meemiy) and reflects a stronger and special form of love than حُبٌّ (Hubb).
- This means that your personality, your upbringing, education and preparation will be manufactured and built under my watch.
Derivative: تَنزِيل is something that has come down over time and bit by bit.

Derivative: غَمٌّ (Ghamm) in Arabic comes from darkness and dark clouds غَمَامٌ (Ghamaam). Sadness is being compared to darkness. غَمٌّ (Ghamm) refers to sadness, uncertainty and darkness i.e., devoid of joy and tranquillity. This can be a reference to a life of imprisonment which Musa (as) would have faced had Allah not saved him from that.
- Then you came right on schedule i.e., at the ordained time.

Derivative: This is the اِفْتَعَلَ (Ifta’ala) form which is stronger in meaning than صَنَعَ (Sana’a) and increased further in strength by the ط in place of the ت. The meaning is: Is to manufacture to perfection. Here Allah is saying that you have turned out exactly as I intended.

Derivative: تَنِيَا comes from تَوانٍ (Tawaanin) the Masdar or تَوَانَى (Tawaanaa) the verb, which means to fall short in something or be slack in fulfilling a responsibility due to lassitude or laziness.

Derivative: لِينَةٌ (Leenah) in Arabic is an extra soft type of date. From it we get the word لِينٌ (Leen) which means softness and flexibility. لَيِّنٌ (Layyin) is an اِسم صِفَة (Ism Sifah) and means constantly soft and flexible. In reference to speech, it means to speak in a nice and gently manner with no harshness in it.

Derivative: There are three verbs. فَرَطَ (Farata), أَفْرَطَ (Afrata) and فَرَّطَ (Farrata). أَفْرَطَ (Afrata) means to exceed the legal limit. فَرَّطَ (Farrata) means to do less than the legal minimum. Either one of these is فَرَطَ (Farata). Here فَرَطَ – يَفْرُطَ means that both meanings are possible: 1) That he (i.e., Fir’awn) reacts to us in an excessive manner or 2) He may not allow us even the chance to present ourselves. Either way he will transgress against us; either by exceeding the bounds or by not even providing them with a chance.
- I hear and I see: The fact that hearing is placed before seeing here indicates to closeness i.e., Allah’s to Musa and Harun (peace and blessings be upon them) and as such they should not fear.

- Two possible meanings: 1) May peace be upon the one who has followed guidance i.e., as a supplication, and 2) The one who follows guidance will attain peace, with the added implication that peace, security and safety are the result of following the guidance and if you “Fir’awn” do not follow guidance then none of that is secured for you.

Derivative: اسْتَوَى - To take one’s place and be settled. It means to be balances, to take charge or rise and take charge. This word was used before in respect to kings taking their throne and their inauguration. The meaning of balance can imply that the king is now stable and settled in his position.
- He (Allah) created it (خَلْقَهُۥ) and guided it (هَدَىٰ). Guidance here means that Allah gave a purpose, direction, instruction, rules and programming for everything that He created.
Derivative: Where حالٌ refers to a situation, بالٌ refers to a current or immediate situation.
- This means: what is the predicament of the former generations?
Derivative: مَهْدًا means cradle. Allah created the original cradle for you which is the earth.

Derivative: سُلوكٌ in Arabic is to stretch something and make it into something else. سَلَكَ here means that Allah stretched and thread out for you all kinds of paths and routes for you in it (i.e., ٱلْأَرْضَ the land).
- Those who possess very sharp intellects. ٱلنُّهَىٰ refers to being able to reach the final conclusion or root and origin of a matter by examining it.
Derivative: تَارَةً means a time. تَارَةً أُخْرَى means another time or once again.
- “And We showed him (i.e., Fir’awn) all the signs” means all the signs that he needed to see and were sufficient for him to see.
Derivative: سُوًى means even and flat, for the greatest visibility. سُوًى can also mean fair and balanced.
Derivative: ضُحًى is the first part of the morning following sunrise.
- Literally it means: “The day of beauty and adornment” but is referring to a day of the festival (Eid) when people adorn themselves and their environment.
Derivative: ٱلثَّرَى- The lowest layer of soil that is moist referring to the depths of the earth.
Derivative: كَيْد means plot or plotting.
- then He came back i.e., to launch His attack.
- He put or brought together his plot.
Derivative: خَابَ refers to the worst possible state where all hope is destroyed: a state of failure and despair.

Derivative: أَسْحَتَ - يُسْحِتُ is from سُحْت which means illicit and Haram money i.e., from bribery, usury, selling Haram things. أَسْحَتَ is when you neglect something until it’s destroyed. The meaning here is that you will be destroyed with the implication that your sources of wealth as a result of your destruction.
Derivative: وَيْلَكُمْ means: May the worst form of destruction be brought upon you. It also means: Curse you people and woe to you.
- they disagreed amongst each other in respect to their affair.
- they kept secret the confidential discussion.
Derivative: مُثْلَى (Muthlaa) is the feminine of أَمْثَل (Amthal) means exemplary; a good or best model and example to be followed and looked up to.
Derivative: ذَهَبَ بِ means to remove, get rid of to be gone with.
- Exemplary and perfect lifestyle – similar to statements such as “our great civilisation” which is a model for the world.
Derivative: اِسْتِعْلَى is used in Arabic to refer to the one who rises above the people. Here it means: “Who will be on top of the people, impress them and win them over”.
Derivative: أَوْجَسَ: To feel something that is hidden inside like a dreading which happens to someone who is afraid and hides his fear deep inside himself.
Derivative: تَلْقَفْ from لَقِفً which means to swallow immediately. It means to swallow quickly and skilfully or efficiently.
- “What they manufactured” which implies that they put a lot of work and effort to produce it (i.e., the magic trick).
Derivative: جَهَرَ -جَهْر means to call or proclaim so loudly that everyone can hear.
- He (Allah) knows the secret and what is more hidden: Secrets are normally known by at least one person whilst what is more hidden is that which is not known by anyone.
Derivative: To crucify meaning to nail you (to the trunks of palm trees).
Derivative: دَرَكٌ means to catch up and overtake. دَرَكٌ also means the bottom or lowest part of the sea.
Derivative: غَشِيَ means to be covered but not made invisible.

here both the literal meaning and its figurative meaning can be applied. fir’awn literally misguided them to the wrong path i.e., the one that led to their physical destruction and figuratively he misled them away from the path of truth versus the path of falsehood.
Derivative: يَحِلَّ means to unleash or to make descend which is in reference to punishment here.
Derivative: هَوَىٰ means to fall from a very high place and crash down.
Derivative: اِهْتَدَى means to commit oneself to guidance i.e., to following it.
Derivative: عَلَىٰٓ أَثَرِى literally means: “Upon my footsteps” which means that they are close by and not far away.
Derivative: مَلْك (Malk) is an intention or decision that we have control over.

- It can mean: “Can they not see that it (i.e., the calf that they are worshipping) cannot respond to them with a word” and “Can they not see that it (i.e., the calf that they are worshipping) cannot respond to a single word”. The sum meaning is that they are incapable of conversing with you.
details.arabic_part: أَفَلَا يَرَوْنَ أَلَّا يَرْجِعُ إِلَيْهِمْ قَوْلًا

details.text: - The present tense is used because although it is in the context of Bani Isra’eel it is also within the context of the Mushrikeen of Makkah and is just as applicable to them.
Derivative: حَدِيثُ means the news or information with the implication that it is new and should be looked at from anew.

Derivative: عَاكِف means in full devotion. العَكْف means to stop and prevent yourself from doing anything else. اِعْتِكاف is when you prevent yourself from other things in terms of leaving on worldly issues and confine yourself to worship in the Masjid.
Derivative: لاَ نَبْرَحُ means will remain and stay.

- It means: “That you did not follow me” with the meaning of leaving the people to catch up with me and inform me about what they’re doing. It can also mean: “That you did not follow my way and manner of dealing with the issue”.

Derivative: خَطْب is used in the Qur’an when a matter or situation doesn’t make sense and you want to find out what’s going on. مَا خَطْبُكَ is like saying: “What’s your issue or deal?” or “What’s your problem?”
Derivative: قَبَضَ is to take hold or grab and قَبْضَةً is a fistful.
Derivative: نَبَذَ means to cast or threw aside something that has no value or worth to you.
Derivative: بَصُرَ means to keep a close watch but also means to be insightful. بَصُرْتُ can therefore mean: “I saw something” with the meaning of “I had an insight”.

Derivative: حَرَّقَ in Arabic means to burn something until it reduces to ashes or small pieces. Burning here of the calf made from gold is so that it can be reduced to small pieces that can then be scattered in the river.
Derivative: مِسَاسَ comes from مَسٌّ which means a touch but the Shaddah (ّ) has been removed making the meaning of مِسَاسَ the least amount of touch.
